entry.title: What are the Add ons in Employee Central?
entry.content:
We are implementing Employee Central. We have the requirement to use contingent workforce in the system as well. But I came to know standard EC do not handle the requirement and an additional component "SAP Fieldglass" would be installed as an add-on. 

Where can I get the list of Add-ons for Employee Central? Are these third party software?

#Fieldglass #Add-ons

entry.title: Issues Transporting Global Scripts to Q
entry.content:
I've completed a Screen Personas build including global scripts in a development environment, and everything is working as intended there. When I moved everything to quality, the global scripts are not ending up linked to each screen. Some were carried over and related to one screen, some were carried over but not related to any screen. 

Are there any recommendations for how to get the whole system working in Quality? 

 

Thanks in advance!
Dan

entry.title: How to determine the date a quote item was last modified?
entry.content:
I looked at CTX tags, scripts, and the database tables - but could not find anything. I have not checked API - maybe there?
Last resort is to add an attribute to EVERY SINGLE PRODUCT with an unconditional rule to set it to TODAY.

Thanks.

entry.title: 如何移转现金账(零用金)
entry.content: 用户会使用"现金帐"功能，请问可以如何移转(开帐)现金帐的期初余额？

entry.title: SAP Design Studio Custom Community components aren't working after BWQ refresh??
entry.content:
Has anyone that is still using SAP Design Studio 1.6 and the custom community components had issues after a PROD to QA copy of data?  Our production system still works but once our basis did a PROD to QA copy of data to get it refreshed our components stopped working with the following error.

I feel it's still pointing to a production system but not sure where that setting would be located to let our basis team know.  We are running design studio on Netweaver and not BO.

"ITEM" of type "ORG_SCN_COMMUNITY_DATABOUND_TABLE2D" could not be generated

 
The extensions are installed on the platform.

We have deployed this app and trying to understand how to best use of the forecast and cross charging section. We do not have separate space for the applications, how can we identify and cross charge, e.g. different projects using same Integration Suite space and sub account. Can we do that via this app? Any idea or best practice you can share?

José

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 


My name is Oluwatobi. I'm from Nigeria. I identify as a person in 2 categories:
- Women
- People looking to switch their careers mid-life.
I worked as a business analyst in a financial institution. However, I recently relocated, and I realized that SAP/ERP skills would make me compete better for high-paying jobs in the job market.

I have started with the SAP for beginners' sections and will hopefully start the SAP for consultants before the deadline.

Thank you for the opportunity

entry.title: Read BW BEX Query from ABAP using FM RRW3_GET_QUERY_VIEW_DATA
entry.content:
I am trying to read data from BW Bex query but i am not able to get the data based on filters i am passing in function module

After Running FM i am getting all the data, it seems that filter are not being applied, if i am doing anything wrong here please let me know, or if there is any other better way to do it let me know.

Here are the screenshots for my input;

entry.title: Re: Workaround to check whitespaces in formula builder in...
entry.content:
I haven't used Quote 2.0 yet. But if it allows <*EVAL()*> tags like in Quote 1.0, that tag supports almost every function on this page: https://www.w3schools.com/asp/asp_ref_vbscript_functions.asp

One useful function there is Replace(String,Find,ReplaceWith). So, instead of your <*CTX( Quote.CustomField(Quote Description))*> tag, you could first modify it like this: 
<*EVAL(Replace(<*CTX( Quote.CustomField(Quote Description))*>," ",""))*>

And then use one of your two methods to see if it's blank.

entry.title: Re: Sign in with an Email link - New feature for SAP Concur login
entry.content: @onorio_neto Thank you for your comment! Email does not need to verified for you to receive the one time link so if you don't receive the one time link then probably the issue is something else

entry.title: Re: How to put the BPM Governance Building Blocks to good use
entry.content:
This blog post truly emphasizes the importance of good governance in Business Process Management (BPM). The building blocks and clearly defined steps provided on how to Setup Business Process Management Initiative will ultimately lead to better organizational performance and long-term success for any corporation.

Great post!

entry.title: Answer to tutorial question in SAP Build
entry.content:
In the SAP Build tutorial, there is a question asking: "In the example, what is the barcode number of the scanned food item (in the picture just above with the mobile phone)?". I have put the answer as 0031200016034 but it tells me that that is incorrect. I have tried other variations such as 031200016034, 0 31200 01603 4 but all are incorrect.

If anyone has completed this tutorial, can you give some suggestions or hints as to what the correct answer is?

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago: SAP Fiori fundamentals, UX transformation, and AI innovations
entry.content:
I’m just returning to the office after hosting our second SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago. The event, held on August 13, 2024; was an exciting opportunity to connect with customers and partners to understand where they are on their user experience journey, as well as show off the latest UX and artificial intelligence innovations from SAP. Colleagues and implementation partners worked together to create a diverse program that included the monetary value of user experience transformation, an introduction to SAP Fiori and the current state of the SAP user experience, the scaling ability of SAP Fiori elements, and AI innovations in app development.

The agenda for SAP Fiori Innovation Day Chicago

Our day began with an introduction to SAP Fiori with Katja Zoch and Sebastian Steinhauer, chief product owners of SAP UX Engineering. It was a fantastic opportunity to come together with the audience and understand what the modern SAP user experience is. The big takeaway: SAP Fiori is more than just the applications, it’s the entire design system that drives how applications and experiences feel across devices. With these terms set, we could now move into deeper, more technical topics.

Giving an introduction to SAP Fiori

After our introduction session, Ioannis Grammatikakis, head of SAP UX Engineering, gave a demo of the current state of the user experience in SAP S/4HANA. It was an overview of how SAP is working to make the experience of using SAP S/4HANA consistent and delightful, collaborative for every user, integrated across devices, and intelligent in all interactions. He demonstrated this by showing how users begin their interactions with SAP in My Home, demonstrated how AI will enhance the experience of using applications by allowing for natural language interactions with the system, and then finished off by showing how SAP integration with Microsoft Teams allows users to work even more collaboratively than ever before.

Demonstrating natural language filtering via Easy Filter for SAP Fiori applications

Our first partner session of the day came from Niklas Winterfeld of Sovanta. His talk The Exponential Costs of Ignoring Users was the first of many sessions that stressed end-user centricity when planning your SAP user experience. It is much cheaper to identify and fix user experiences challenges in the discovery and design stages than after you have deployed your applications.

After the important reminders about the value of a good user experience, Ioannis Grammatikakis turned our attention to extensibility options for SAP S/4HANA in the cloud – demonstrating how it can still be flexible when needed for your use case. He walked through options for both SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The big takeaway: there are options for extending SAP S/4HANA that allow you to keep your core clean, allowing for easy upgrades. For on-stack extensibility, there are options for key users as well as developers via ABAP Cloud. For side-by-side extensibility using SAP BTP, there are options involving SAP Build Code.

The last session of the morning was a forward-looking demo, using SAP Fiori tools with AI to generate an SAP Fiori elements application from a sketch of business requirements that was created in the room with help from the audience. My colleague Thorsten Lange and I were excited to show this off as many customers and partners ask for the best way to create a quick first draft of an application that they can use to test with users and innovate further. This demo was an extension of the one our team showed at SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Dublin, the big difference being that we showed how you will be able to use SAP Fiori tools to update the application you generated – adding new columns to the list report via SAP Fiori tools and using the Flexible Programming Model to create a custom layout for one of the pages. You’re not limited to the output of SAP Fiori tools AI. If you’re interested in generating an app with SAP Fiori tools AI, the first version – which allows you to generate an app from a written business requirement document – is available.

SAP Fiori tools AI turned this whiteboard sketch into a running list report application

Attendees returned after lunch to a panel discussion on UX transformation. Gavin Quinn of Mindset, Thomas Bechberger of Sovanta, Dayna Nielsen of ConvergentIS, and SAP’s Ioannis Grammatikakis discussed how to best position and execute UX improvements in an organization. The discussion covered a wide range of topics from the initial change management, to scoping your project, to rolling out the updated experience. The golden thread at the center of it all? You have to take your end-users with you every step of the way. Managing their expectations, understanding their needs as the people actually using your software, and keeping them informed of the changes are key to a successful UX transformation.

The panel discussion was followed by another partner session, this time by Mindset’s Gavin Quinn. He shared the story of how Mindset drove a successful user experience transformation for a utility company; driven by consistent communications with end-users to understand how they could best be upskilled and transitioned to the new experience. He also shared an exciting story of how the company integrated an AI assistant into SAP Fiori to simplify the customer support experience for customer service representatives. It was refreshing to see a real-world example of AI simplifying a business process rather than just being shown off for the sake of showing off the technology.

Our final partner session of the day was hosted by ConvergentIS. Deep Bhandari and Dayna Nielsen shared how SAP Fiori elements can be used to deliver consistent applications quickly; telling a story around how ConvergentIS used the solution at scale to deliver 12 applications in 2.5 months, a 66% time savings over freestyle development.

After all of the content on SAP Fiori development, attendees had a dedicated hour to create an SAP Fiori elements application of their own. Using SAP Business Application Studio and SAP Fiori tools, participants got hands-on with the solution, creating an Incident Management app with SAP Fiori elements for OData V4. Most of the participants were able to complete the exercise in less than one hour. If you would like to try it out yourself, you can follow the tutorial here.

The day wrapped up with some quick demos of planned AI innovations from SAP UX Engineering.

  • We showed off how users can automatically populate their new My Home for SAP S/4HANA Cloud pages using AI recommendations. This ensures that users get a good start with applications and insight cards that are relevant to them.
  • We also showed how users can generate their own Insight cards using AI by asking Joule for exactly what they need.
  • Finally, we gave a quick demo of Collaboration Manager, illustrating how users could directly work together in SAP Fiori applications.

SAP Fiori Innovation Day Chicago was a fantastic opportunity to understand our customers’ and partners’ user experience priorities as well as share some of the most cutting edge solutions coming out of SAP. I look forward to seeing where the next one takes us! Is there somewhere you’d like to see a similar event? What content would you like to see there? Feel free to let us know in the comments.

If you want to learn more about these topics:

  • Subscribe to our SAP Fiori development newsletter, which covers most of the topics we discusses at SAP Fiori Innovation Day.
  • Join other SAP Fiori developers from a variety of companies in our monthly SAP Fiori elements roundtable.
  • Watch the SAP Community and LinkedIn for information about when a SAP Fiori Innovation Day will be in your area.

entry.title: Re: Step Into Autonomy: Introduction to Configuration Manager
entry.content: Excellent content!

entry.title: Midpoint Scheduling in SAP Digital Manufacturing Resource Orchestration
entry.content:
What is Midpoint Scheduling?

Midpoint Scheduling, also known as bottleneck scheduling, is a feature of the Resource Orchestration (REO) Dispatch and Monitoring app. It allows you to designate a single resource as a reference point—typically a bottleneck—and use the operation(s) planned for this resource as the basis for scheduling all other operations around it.

In this method, operation scheduling is anchored to the end date of the operation(s) planned for the midpoint resource. All preceding operations are scheduled backward, while all subsequent operations are scheduled forward. This approach simplifies the scheduling process by using the bottleneck resource or operation as the central reference point for the entire order.

How it works?

To easily demonstrate this functionality, I created several production orders with the following routing:

Which are planned for the very same time frame, in parallel:

If I dispatch them all, simply using the pre-existing 'Find Slot' and 'Forward' direction options:

This is the end result:

However, since I have only one resource of type 'RES3' in my plant, which I already know is currently my bottleneck, I prefer to schedule all its operations first, and then derive the schedule for all other operations from it. This can be easily achieved by leveraging the new 'Backward/Midpoint' scheduling option:

Note that, as soon as the brand new 'Backward/Midpoint' scheduling option is selected, a new icon is enabled on the Resources icon bar, which allows you to set the midpoint (bottleneck) resource:

Which in my case is 'RES3', so I set it as the midpoint.

Now, if I dispatch the operations once again, this time using 'Backward/Midpoint' scheduling option and having 'RES3' set as the midpoint resource, this is what we get:

Where we can see that all 'OP03' operations were dispatched to 'RES3' first, and all the preceding and subsequent other operations were dispatched afterwards, while referencing 'OP03' schedule.

To better understand and visualize it, let´s check the 'Last Scheduling Results' report, where we can see that, across all orders, all 'OP03' operations were scheduled backwards in sequence on 'RES3' resource, and that within each order, all other operations were scheduled based on the 'OP03' schedule.

It´s worth mentioning that, when using Midpoint Scheduling, if an operation schedule falls in the past (prior to the current date and time, aka as NOW), the affected orders are automatically grouped in a Variant for the Operation Worklist panel with the prefix 'AUTO', so they can be easily found and scheduled later on using a different methodology or approach.

For more details, please check: Scheduling Options.

 

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP: Error: Can't execute query as no primary database is connected.
entry.content: I faced the same problem. I could solve it. You should update sap/cds to the newest version. Currently "@sap/cds": "^8.1.1". sap/cds-dk dependency should be updated too (or removed)

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Product compliance - Safety Data Sheet creat...
entry.content:
Hello

i propsose to check SAP marketplace. You will find there a clear roadmap for "on premise" and "cloud" looking to the future (there exsits a OSS note in which the road map is described): BUT any roadmap is documenting only the "current status" of progress and the roadmap can change at any piont in time

Regarding your question

1.) in On premise S/4 hana release 2023 you can upload SDS and sent SDS to the customer (deatails. check On line help)

2.) The "data model" to handle SDS data is not final now; this will take some releases to complete; and later SAP will deploy an option to generate in the system a SDS

In a nutshell: Check status of the software wih SAP. If you would like to use the software soon... you should use "cloud version". Here you well get much faster new functionality.

The next on prem version can be expected roughly in november 2025.

SAP is currently creating here many new blogs regarding the software

C.B.

PS: Check e.g. https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-members/about-product-compliance-on-premise-version-2023-introduction-for-beginners/ba-p/13739600

Many thanks

entry.title: Re: UNKNOW ERROR building web app
entry.content: same error

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
Re: Cancel Goods Receipt PO query report into SAP Busines...
entry.content:
Hi,

You may wish to utilize the following query for identifying cancelled Goods Receipts:

SELECT T0."DocEntry", T0."DocNum", T0."CardCode", T0."CardName", T0."NumAtCard", T1."LineNum", T1."ItemCode", T1."Dscription", T1."Quantity" FROM OPDN T0 INNER JOIN PDN1 T1 ON T0."DocEntry" = T1."DocEntry" WHERE T0."CANCELED" = 'Y' AND T0."ObjType" = 20 AND T0."DocDate" BETWEEN [%0] and [%1]

Best of luck.

entry.title: Re: Contabilizar valor líquido
entry.content: Quando o item não é estocável não funciona @rodrigofeula, quando o item é estocável funciona mesmo colocando no depósito 99.

entry.title: Q2 2024 Release Webcasts: What’s New in SAP Customer Experience
entry.content:
SAP Customer Experience (CX) wrapped up the first half of 2024 with many exciting new features. The features continue the Intelligent CX themes of Industry-tailored, Connected, Insightful, and Adaptive that were introduced at last year’s SAP Sapphire.

During this year’s SAP Sapphire, we highlighted the Intelligent CX portfolio and roadmap and showcased how SAP is leading the way in Business AI. Rewatch the many CX highlights here.

Also, sign up for the virtual SAP CX Live event happening on September 24 to see how our solutions help your customers get more of what they want!

Now, on to the Q2 2024 release recaps.

SAP Commerce Cloud
Learn about the new “Ask the product” feature in SAP CX AI Toolkit that leverages Generative AI to answer shopper questions right on the storefront. SAP CX AI Toolkit also expanded the Visual Search capability beyond retail to now include all industries.

We’ve also made it easier for customers to self-scale their SAP Commerce Cloud environments with scalable sandboxes and purchasing consumption credits, a virtual currency. SAP Commerce Cloud also added canary deployments to allow customers to test blue-green deployments by transferring a certain percentage of live traffic to the green environment.

Learn about these and other features in this webcast.
Watch webcast replay

SAP Sales and Service Cloud – 2408 Release Briefing Webcast
The August 2024 (Release 2408) of SAP Sales & Service Cloud has many new updates.

For Dynamic Visit Planning, appointments with locations will be shown as map pins, enhancing the visibility. Clicking anywhere on an appointment card will display a popover with pertinent appointment details.

For Agent Desktop, social media messages, including WhatsApp, can be displayed in the Chat Activities and Timeline, making it easier to track customer interactions.

We also have a Readiness Check Tool and Data Transfer Tool to make for a smooth transition from Sales and Service Cloud V1 to V2.

Learn about these and other features in this webcast.
Watch webcast replay

SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 - Utilities and SAP Enterprise Service Management: Q2 2024 Release Overview

We’ve added more AI enhancements. Case summaries can be created using Generative AI and can be viewed in the Customer Hub in card or table view. Another new feature is a machine learning model that analyzes all email content during email to case conversion and determines the Case Type.

The SAP Service Cloud Utilities Add-on has seen many new updates, from Financials, Contract Account, Payment Plans, and Premise objects to provide a holistic view for customer service reps.

SAP Enterprise Service Management is the one service management environment for all lines of business. It is built for modern shared service organizations, with embedded process automation capabilities, and out-of-the-box integration into SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Business Networks, and more. SAP Enterprise Service Management has enhanced Agent Desktop to support employee and supplier scenarios.

Learn about these and other features in this webcast.
Watch the V2 webcast here

SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement June 2024 Product Release
The Emarsys June 2024 product release strengthened an already robust AI feature set, including the new AI Product Finder, AI Preheader Generator, and AI Segment Generator. These features help marketers to quickly create compelling content that delivers relevant experiences at scale.

SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement also added new marketing and advertising channels, enhanced analytics, collaborative marketing tools, and much more.

Learn about these and other features in this webcast.
Watch webcast replay

This was a brief summary on some of the new features so far. Look for many more exciting features in the second half this year!

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2024.001 Versão 1.10: Atualizações recentes na Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e)
entry.content: @Eduardolarrion descobri que o problema está apenas no PR(vi que seu exemplo era desse estado também), testei em uma filial de SP e funcionou corretamente, em outras atualizações o PR já teve esses problemas de modificar o XML de retorno e dava erros no GRC, vamos avaliar melhor e entrar em contato com a SEFAZ.

entry.title: Re: Customer Payment Upload Program or Multiple Receipts...
entry.content:
Hello,

you can upload Customer invoice via "Upload Customer Open Items" app or there are also other options (e.g. via API) as well. For more information, please see:

2639713 - Import Customer Invoices - SAP for Me

To upload payments files, you can use "Manage Incoming Payment Files" app

How to Import Payment Files | SAP Help Portal

Regards,

Yanica

entry.title: Re: Issues Transporting Global Scripts to Q
entry.content:
• Ensure that you are on the latest Personas SP and all notes are implemented in both the source and target system according to the Health Check
  • Run the 'Update object relations' function in the source system before transporting. Also, for good measure, do the same in the target system if you are still having problems with missing global scripts or linkages.
  • Double-check that the global script your flavor is referencing is indeed not present in the target system (in other words, it has not been transported along with your flavor).
  • If so (but please make sure this is indeed the case), create a support incident and provide the necessary access to both the source and target system, describing all details to reproduce the issue (like the global script ID, which local script or control is it supposed to be linked to etc.)

entry.title: Re: Data Analyzer URL Parameter
entry.content: URL API guide shows that we can pass many values for a single dimension in the formal ["ST1","ST10"], but it does not seem to work. Data analyzer interprets our request as if a single value [ST1,ST10] was passed. Any guess how can we pass an array of values?

entry.title: Re: Are chatbots still supported or recommended for SAP B...
entry.content:
Hi @GeorgeAbraham,

Regarding "Wouldn’t it be a little glitchy or like tampering with the flow?"  Yes, exactly and it is for this reason I do not recommend building a custom bot.  As consultants we need to act as a trusted advisor and resist the temptation to say YES all the time.  In this case, I think the customer's expectation needs to be set that lack of chatbot support is a current limitation of Work Zone.  Building a custom bot that provides a meaningful user experience will require significant development effort and ongoing maintenance, let alone that it would not be a solution supported by SAP.


Since the "customer is more keen on a preconfigured guided bot", my recommendation would be to try implementing SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone Guided Experiences.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Screen Personas - Field cannot be moved
entry.content: Is your system fully up to date with all notes implemented (all green), according to the Health Check?

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2024.001 Versão 1.10: Atualizações recentes na Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e)
entry.content:
@jonhycarlos 

Realmente pela filial SP aqui tbm deu certo, vou acionar a sefaz do PR tambem.

Gratidao man!

entry.title: Re: SlipStream Issue with QM02

entry.title: What jobs are relevant to the Migration Cockpit?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

While using the Migrate Your Data - Migration Cockpit Fiori app, we've faced some long running activities and tasks in it, but thanks to some KBAs/SAP Notes and the blog post below, the issue has been resolved:

  • Troubleshooting long running Data Migration Jobs.

In the Step 1 of the expert post above though, only two jobs related to the Migration Cockpit were mentioned:

  • /LTB/JOB_DISPATCHER
  • /LTB/RETENTION

What we'd like to confirm then is the following:

  • Are the two jobs mentioned above (/LTB/JOB_DISPATCHER and /LTB/RETENTION) the only jobs associated to the Migration Cockpit? Or should other jobs also be reviewed?

Thanks for the support!

Best regards,
Data migration expert team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: What jobs are relevant to the Migration Cockpit?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

The statement of the expert in the post is correct: only the jobs /LTB/JOB_DISPATCHER and /LTB/RETENTION need to be maintained and reviewed if your purpose is to guarantee that the Migration Cockpit will work correctly.

The /LTB/JOB_DISPATCHER job will be responsible for general activities and tasks triggered by the Migration Cockpit, while the /LTB/RETENTION job, if set up, will delete retained information of finished Migration Projects. More information about /LTB/RETENTION can be found in the following SAP Note:

  • 2997667 - SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit – Deleting projects using retention period

I hope this solves your doubt! Good luck with your future Migration Projects!

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to activate 2LIS_03_BF in RSA5
entry.content: Yes, it does appear in both transactions that you mentioned, but it continues to indicate with the x icon, as if it were disabled

entry.title: Usage of TO_DECIMAL and SUM together
entry.content:
I came across some SQL code that uses the 2 expressions TO_DECIMAL and SUM together. In some cases, the TO_DECIMAL is used first (followed by SUM) and other cases it's the other way around. 

Case A: TO_DECIMAL (SUM (xyz), 25, 10)

Case B: SUM( TO_DECIMAL(xyz, 25, 10) )

I'm uncertain if there is any difference in the result depending on which way you sequence them? Which sequence is recommended?

Hi to all 

is there any update by SAP about  SOwen_SAP-SF comment above ?

 

many thanks !

entry.title: Create Alternate GL Hierarchies in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
I have been asked by team members to create some alternate hierarchies within our GL Account dimension.  For example, let's say we have a hierarchy node that includes GL Accounts 100001, 100002, 100003.  Can I create an alternate node that includes just 100001 and 100002 and excludes 100003?  For the time being I have created an alternate hierarchy that sits next to our standard hierarchy (see below) but it would be much more convenient if I could do it all within the same hierarchy so the teams didn't have to switch hierarchies when reporting or looking at data.

Within what below is called "Account Hierarchy" I can create the node that is needed but I can't duplicate where the base GL Accounts roll up to.  This was easy to do in BPC and wanted to see if it was possible in SAP Analytics Cloud?

Best regards,

Norman

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: Please find the below solution:

entry.title: Issues with an INT64 parameter in a Com UFL..
entry.content:
I have a COM UFL written in Visual Basic.  Been working for years with CR2016 designer and SP34 of the Crystal Reports for Visual Studio.  32 bit.  My database has had a INT64 (DBTYPE_I8) column added to it, and I'd like to do some custom formatting with this value.  My reports can read the column just fine, display it as a number, etc, but I want to pass it to a UFL function as a 64 bit int.  In Visual Basic, visual studio 2019, a Long datatype is an INT64.  So I added the world's simplest function to the UFL:

Public Function TestLong(ByVal value as Long) As String  

 TestLong := "TestLong"

End Function

I'm not calling this function in the report, but it is in the dll.  I regasm'd it, copied it hither and yon, etc etc just like always.  Yet the mere presence of this function causes my DLL not to load.  In CR2016 I get the 'saved data will be discarded since one or more formulas failed to compile' and from CRforVS, at runtime, I get the infamous 'UFL u212com.dll that implements this function is missing'.

Obviously Crystal is not liking my UFL with this function using a Long parameter.

I recompiled as 64bit, regasm'ing the 64 bit version, and used CR2022.  Same results.  If I take this function out of the dll, all is well.  Put it in, fails.

I also changed Long to Int64 in the VB code to see if that made any difference.  Same results.

Anyone have any ideas?  Anyone using a COM UFL with a function taking an INT64 parameter?

entry.title: Accounting Activity Allocation API
entry.content:
I want to post a payload via an integration flow. May someone help with preparing an XSD file that will work for the Accounting Activity Allocation API. I have done a post via Insomnia, and it was successfully. Here is an example of the payload that I posted. 

/ActivityAllocation
{
"ControllingArea": "Text Here",
"FiscalYear": "2024",
"AccountingDocumentHeaderText": "Text Here",
"_Item": [
{
"SenderCostCenter": "SCC",
"PartnerCostCenter": "PCC",
"DocumentItemText": "DIT",
"Quantity": 0,
"BaseUnit": "KM",
"CostCtrActivityType": "CCAT"
}
]
}

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Application Log as Launchpad or V4 Odata serv...

Hi Peter,

true, but this can only display a single log, and you have to know the loghandle. Searching for logs etc. is not possible. So the question is: why is that 1487 app not available in BTP steampunk?

Best regards
Holm

entry.title: SAP Best Practices for Warehousing
entry.content:
SAP EWM Implementation Template

In this blog, I will highlight the Best Practices available for SAP S/4HANA EWM, focusing on how templates have sped up the implementation process of SAP products. 

The points I will cover include: 

  • What are Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA EWM?
  • What's in the Best Practice Content?
  • Where to find the Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA EWM?

These are some of the business benefits of using a template:

  • Reduce Total cost of implementation
  • Reduce cost of operations by using standardized processes delivered by SAP Best Practices
  • Drive operational efficiencies
  • Comply with regulations
  • Allow a first-hand experience of SAP S/4HANA with instant guided trials

Warehouse Structure

 

What are Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA EWM?

Best Practice content has been available from at least 2010 with the Rapid Deployment Solution(RDS). Best Practice documentation provides templates to assist in the implementation of SAP S/4HANA EWM. Best Practices are available for customers and partners with a valid SAP S/4HANA license. The documents are standardised across different processes from Inbound to Outbound. 

         

 

What's in the Best Practice Content?

  • Process Flows

A detailed process flow is available across ERP and EWM with user roles, this is extremely useful to customers that want to standardise their processes. 

  • Accelerators

Test scripts are delivered to guide unit testing and end-user testing. 

  • Roles

Predefined Roles are available for all warehouse processes, this takes reduces the workload during implementation of authorization team.

  • Master Data, Organizational Data, and Other Data

The organizational structure reflects the structure of an example company. The master data represents materials, customers, and suppliers, depending on the operational focus of the demonstrated processes. The business process is enabled with this organization-specific master data.

Where to find the Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA EWM?

The Best Practice content can be found at SAP4ME. This is a link to the main content for SAP S/4HANA 2022. These notes can be used to help you understand the the content better and what is needed to get started. 

SAP note: 2993430

This note gives an overview about the available Best Practice Content for the different deployment options of SAP EWM

SAP note: 2782080

Overview of Guides or Best Practices Documents for Extended Warehouse Management

SAP note: 1606493

 For details about the best practices for  deployment options.

SAP note: 2483936

Additional  Information on SAP Best Practices for S/4 Hana – Supply Chain Management for Extended Warehouse Management.

Conclusion

SAP Best Practice is a great way to start your warehousing project by reducing the effort required to get a warehouse up and running. The processes may not suit your business requirements 100% and may need to be adjusted, these changes will probably require minimum effort from the project team. 

I hope this blog helped will help reduce the effort to implement SAP EWM. If you have any questions, please feel free to post it below or leave a comment.
In my next blog I plan to give an overview of the Best Practice content for SAP TM. Thank you for reading my blog, please share your feedback in the comments section.

For more updates on SAP Extended Warehouse Management please visit

https://community.sap.com/topics/extended-warehouse-management

If you have any questions on Extended Warehouse Management please visit https://answers.sap.com/index.html

entry.title: Migration of Custom Approve Purchase Contracts App (F0400) from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear folks.
I am writing to request technical support regarding the migration of a customized extension based on the standard Approve Purchase Contracts application (F0400) to SAP S/4HANA. The application has undergone extensive customizations and we require guidance on the feasibility and process for its successful migration.
We would appreciate it if you could provide us with the following information:

Migration Guidance: Is there a comprehensive guide or set of instructions available for migrating customized applications like ours to SAP S/4HANA? If so, please provide links or references to these resources.

Migration Feasibility: Can someone confirm whether it is technically feasible to migrate our customized Approve Purchase Contracts application (F0400) to SAP S/4HANA? If there are any potential challenges or limitations, please elaborate on them.

Migration Process: If the migration is feasible, please outline the general steps involved in the process. This would help us understand the timeline, resource requirements, and any specific actions we need to take on our end.

Additional Resources: Can someone direct us to any additional resources, such as SAP Notes, community forums, or support articles, that might be relevant to our specific situation?

We are eager to embark on this migration journey and appreciate your prompt assistance in providing us with the necessary guidance.

Additional information:

  • SAP Application version: EhP 5 for SAP ERP 6.0 with SPS 5 (SAP_APPL 605 - 5)
  • SAP NetWeaver / ABAP Version: SAP EhP2 FOR SAP Netweaver 7.0 with SPS 8 (SAP_BASIS 702 - 😎
  • Database version: ORACLE 12.1
  • Target system: SAP S/4HANA 2023 initial shipment package

Thank you.

Kind regards,
Christian Manrique

entry.title: Massachusetts - Supplemental vs Base Wages
entry.content:
Hello -

We have come across an issue with Massachusetts income tax withholding calculations where regular wages and supplemental wages are being paid at the same time.  We posted a ticket with SAP who told us we needed to remove our supplemental wage type from the "Base wages for BSI" cumulation.  This is a concern for us since all of our taxable wage types (regular or supplemental) are configured with this cumulation and have been since the implementation of SAP.

Does anyone else have a similar issue or similar configuration?  Your insight would be most helpful and appreciated.

Thanks,

Alicia

entry.title: Fixing Screenshot Size and Border while publishing simulations as documents in SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
We have edited and published simulations in SAP Enable Now. Next, we want to publish these simulations as Help Documents in Word Format. We successfully set the Documentation template. In the documentation template, I already set the screenshot scaling to 100%, as shown in the picture below. 

Now, when we publish the simulation as Word document, the size of each screenshot is not full in the document. To make it full size, I need to manually set the image size % as 100 on each step in the simulation, as shown in the picture below.

Here is a sample Word output that shows the difference between the screenshot size with 100% and other not being being 100%.

My question - Is there a way in SAP Enable Now settings to set the image size as 100% by default for all the steps in a simulation; so that I do not have to do it manually? I tried different options, including crop, unprop in bulk, etc. but no success. Please help

entry.title: Loading inception-to-date balances
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am aware of this roadmap item: Ability to enter or load reported data for dimension totals .

As we go live with GR in 2025 and we run on S4H PCE, we need to have a workaround, as the above roadmap item will not yet be available I believe.

Can anyone think of a workaround until then? Our entities provide a full inception-to-date balance sheet (w/o movements) and YTD P/L balances. We need to get this into Group Reporting. The loading process needs to transform these inception-to-date balances into periodic deltas with transaction type 915 by substracting the 900 opening balances stored in GR.

We plan to use GRDC, but would be open for any other method that can fulfill our requirement.

Regards,

Morten

Neto

entry.title: Sales Order Invoice Status must be finished manually for items linked to a Project task
entry.content:
For project management, we first enter a sales order and generate a project from it. When the entire process is completed, the project has been completed, and the sales order has been consumed, the sales order still has the status "In Process" and must be manually set to Finished through "Finish Invoicing" button. Can't this status be set automatically? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Sales Order Invoice Status must be finished manually...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

According to the KBA 3050995 - Sales Order is in Status In Process but you Expect it to be in Status Completed:

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Sales Order Item tab has two important statuses:

Delivery Status/Fulfillment Status and Invoice Status.

In case of Sales Orders with Items linked to Project Tasks, the Delivery Status/Fulfillment Status and Invoice status has to be set manually to finished in Project because these are based on actual time recorded and the actuals could be equal or  more than the planned amount in the Sales Order.

In this case the Delivery Status is Finished when the assigned Project Task is Complete and the Invoice status is changed to Finish Invoicing.

Resolution

In case of Sales Orders with Items linked to Project Tasks:

Follow below steps to change the Invoice status of the sales order to finished:

  1. Go to the Customer Invoicing work center.
  2. Select the Project Invoicing view.
  3. Select the Sales Orders sub view.
  4. Select sales order ABC (ABC represents the sales order number).
  5. Press the Change Status button and select the Finish Invoicing button.

 Once  above is done then the Status of the related sales order ABC changes to Completed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Best Regards,

Marcelo
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: Power Designer Repository - SQL QUERY and repository...
entry.content:
Hi,

check Repository Metamodel in your Examples folder. That should help. Be prepared to create a hell of a queries to the database, because you require a lot of information, the location of which is not exactly intuitive in the PD repository. In case you need help with that, feel free to contact me (direct message here or LinkedIn). PowerDesigner customizations and scripting has been my daily bread for the past 16 years.

Regards,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: Subcontracting for special stocks (project) batch determination
entry.content:
Dear gurus,

I would need your help in the following scenario:

- Special subcontracting scenario with account assignment "Q" and item category "L"

- Both header material and component are set as Individual/Coll. Req = 1(Individual requirements only) in MRP4 view. 

- Due to the above setting we are not sending the components to the subcontractor via ME2ON instead the components are kept under special stock, project stock under the project for which subcontracting order is also created.

- With this set up component are getting consumed from project stock when we do GR for the subcontracting order.

- The component is marked as batch managed and when GR is done to receive the component under project stock batch is created automatically

- Batch is not automatically assigned to the component in the subcontracting order and this is causing issue because when we try to GR the subcontracting order we get error "Enter batch"

My question (I am newby for batch management )

- When and how batch assignment to the components should happen? I know this can be done manually in the subcontracting order

- Can batch assignment to the component be automated? If yes should be happen when subcontracting order is created and the BOM is exploded or it should be done during posting GR?

- If batch assignment can be automated can you please provide step by step instruction how I can achieve it?

I really appreciate all inputs on this topic.

Thanks in advance.

 

MAN Batch Management

entry.title: Change revision name of the current revision level for a material
entry.content:
Hi all, do you know if it is possible to change the revision name of the current revision level of a material without changing the revision level itself?  

Thanks,

Diego

entry.title: Re: How to add rows with value in ALP
entry.content: Do you have news about that?

entry.title: Re: Hardware key for the sap system.
entry.content:
Log on to the web System landscape controll center 

i.e https://servername:40000/LicenceController and get the hardware key

server name is on the about or windows system info like: WIN-SHGF622JR

entry.title: Re: #1 - How I configured the integration between SAP TD and SAP TM modules in 8 steps
entry.content: Marcos, Parabéns pela iniciativa, e muito importante para a comunidade esse compartilhamento de conhecimento.

entry.title: In Signavio Process Manager,When will directory-link removal at a collective level be available?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Signavio Process Modeling and Value Accelerator Teams.

I have some questions related to the leveraging of the Value Accelerator "Process Landscape and EA Design" to speed up the Process Modelling and also to remain as aligned as possible to the Process Decomposition and Naming convention in the E2E Value Chains, Business Value Flow Diagrams and Solution Value Flow Diagrams.

I present my analysis first and the questions come at the end.

Example E2E Process: Source to Pay

Example Modelling Scenario:

It has been decided that for Source to Pay E2E processes, it is very important to stay as close as possible to SAP landscape, in accordance to Clean Core, thus benchmarking all levels of decomposition in Source to Pay, its level 2s, Level 3s and L4s(Business Activity Sequences) should be leveraged.

Action:

To create copies of the VAL Diagrams in order to re-use the elements as much as possible.

The below screenshot displays that the Source to Pay Diagram from  VAL was used as benchmark and a New VC Diagram was created. The Elements were selected from the VAL Source to Pay file, copied and pasted into the new VC Diagram.

(Note: creating a copy (save copy) of the original VAL diagram creates the same condition as Copying and Pasting Elements.

 As shown above, each of the Level 2s, Modular Processes in the VC Diagram keep the Directory links and the attributes filled in, in accordance to the VAL taxonmy.

Although the elements decomposition, and Process Elememnt naming convention will be re-used, an organizaiton might necesseraly want to use the exact Directory Entry and attribute naming convention as the VAL.

Thus it is decide to use the standard naming attributes, the organizations naming for dictionary entries and perhaps only re use some attributes from the VAL.

Thus, a Process Developer can remove the links from elements, see screen below:

as seen above, each individual element ( in this case Collapsed Process) needs to be selected so the Directory icon is visible. 

In a case like this, where many elements across the L2, L3 and L4 decompositio will be benchmarked, but not the VAL attributes, a modeller would be able to speed up the creating of the Diagrams and clean up of the new Directory by removing links faster.

Currently, selecting multiple elements does not allow for collective removal. see screen shot below. 

Questions:

1 -Is there a work-around to accomplish the above?

2 -Would collective removal of dictionary links be availble when copying VAL diagrams? Via a tool bar operator?

3 - Would there be a possibility in the future to keep certain VAL directory links and attributes, that is a selective removal? 

For example, a few VAL attributes were considered relevant to re-use? ( Note: Currently removing the Directory Link, takes away all attributes, thus the modeller would have to reselect them again for those that considered could remain )

In the screen below I present an example, of what attributes could be considered benefical to keep using exactly as the VAL attribue:

  • isElementOfRBABPMBusinessProcessType
  • hasComponentRBABPMbusinessprocesssequence
  • identifier

Thanks in advance for your feedback. please feel free to get back to me if you need more information.

Cheers!

entry.title: FSM Activity Detail Screen Not Showing Skills
entry.content:
While on the Dispatching Board tab in FSM under Planning And Dispatching menu option, when displaying the details of an Activity, the Skills required for that activity show under the Skills tab of the detail screen, as well as the side panel, under Skills.

However, when choosing the Activities tab under the Planning and Dispatching menu, and displaying the details of an Activity, the skills do not show under the Skills tab.

Any thoughts on this would be appreciated.  Could possibly be a bug.
